name: Yoo et al. DREsS CASE Synthetic Rubric Labeling Extract
population_or_context: Rubric-based automated essay scoring for English as a foreign language writing.
sample_size: DREsS includes 2,279 real classroom essays, 6,515 standardized existing-dataset samples, and 40,185 synthetic CASE corrupted essays.
methods: Extracted how synthetic corrupted essays are assigned rubric-aligned score ranges and used as training data while real classroom essays remain validation/test evidence.
education_context: Undergraduate EFL argumentative writing scored on content, organization, and language rubric dimensions.
response_origin: Synthetic DREsS_CASE corrupted essays are combined with real and standardized essay sources for training augmentation.
rubric_label_space: Rubric-based content, organization, and language scores.
label_source: Synthetic CASE labels are generated through corruption aligned with target score ranges; real and standardized essays use human or existing-dataset labels.
generation_model: CASE corruption strategy rather than an LLM response generator.
generation_method: Synthetic essays are created by corrupting essays to create lower-quality samples aligned with target score ranges.
prompt_strategy: No LLM prompt strategy; synthetic generation uses a corruption method tied to rubric score ranges.
evaluation_method: Automated essay scoring models are evaluated with rubric-score agreement metrics, while real DREsS_New data is reserved for validation and testing.
scorer_use: Rubric-based AES training and augmentation; not synthetic-only scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: Real classroom essays in DREsS_New provide train, validation, and test evidence and separate real evaluation from synthetic training augmentation.
reported_metrics: The paper reports 40,185 DREsS_CASE synthetic samples and uses weighted kappa for AES evaluation.
limitations: The synthetic responses are used for training, not to validate a scorer as a standalone synthetic test set.
CASE corruption differs from LLM-generated student response simulation.
The evidence is bounded to EFL essay scoring and the DREsS rubric structure.
